_note: Part-7 cascade retrofit of lighting-layout/examples/uk-bathroom-zone-1-zone-2 — replaces the D.2 synthetic helper (_synthetic_photometric_intent.json). 2 × IPx4 LED downlights at 1500 lm each in a 5.67 m² UK domestic bathroom; 200 lux target per BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.36 bathroom_domestic. Happy-path cascade — all 9 INVs PASS. 10-sample slice from the 30-point grid; full grid emitted by runtime calc.lumen_grid_solver.

consumed_summary._layout_note: D.2 special-locations bathroom example: 2 IPx4 LED downlights at (1350, 1050) and (1350, 350) on the 1350 mm centreline; both luminaires sit within BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701 zones at the 2250 mm ceiling boundary. Single 6 A Type B MCB on DB L1; 30 mA RCD per §701.411.3.3.

ies_files._source: Synthetic LED downlight per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 archetype; representative IPx4 ceiling-recessed downlight commonly used in domestic UK bathrooms. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — generated by scripts/generate_reference_ies.py. Engineer-of-record must substitute project IES before final design freeze. Lumen output scaled to 1500 lm per fixture to match upstream lighting-layout LED_DOWNLIGHT_IPX4 declaration.

grid_metadata.grid_spacing_formula: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §6.2 adaptive: p = 0.2 × 5^log₁₀(d) × 1000 clamped [50, 1000] mm; for d = 2.3 m longest task-area side, p ≈ 0.2 × 5^0.362 × 1000 ≈ 356 mm → snapped UP to 450 mm nominal to fit 6 × 5 = 30 grid points exactly across the bathroom task area. 200 mm border substituted for the §4.3 default 500 mm because the room is too small for the 500 mm convention (would leave a 1700×1100 mm task area with degenerate spacing).

reflectances._source: CIBSE SLL Code for Lighting 2012 standard reflectance table for low-RI tiled bathroom: ceiling 0.5 (gloss-painted), walls 0.5 (light tiled), floor 0.2 (mid-tone tile). Matches upstream lighting-layout assumptions[0] (UF = 0.60 at RI = 0.79).

rationale.chat_summary: Photometric verification of the D.2 Part-7 bathroom lighting-layout. 2 × LED_DOWNLIGHT_IPX4 panels (1500 lm each, synthetic IES) deliver achieved_avg 254 lux against the 200 lux bathroom_domestic target (BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.36); achieved_min 168 lux ≥ 140 (0.7 × 200) threshold; U₀ 0.66 ≥ 0.4 bathroom target; max UGR 18.2 ≤ 19 limit. All 9 INVs PASS. Cascade payload sets task_area_compliant=true for upstream lighting-layout INV-11 — closes the D.2 synthetic helper substitution.

sections.title: Cascade context — Part-7 retrofit
sections.summary: Consumes electrical/lighting-layout/examples/uk-bathroom-zone-1-zone-2/intent-out.json — the D.2 special-locations bathroom example. The lighting-layout example shipped pointing INV-11 at a synthetic helper file (_synthetic_photometric_intent.json) because no Part-7 cascade source existed at the time. This cascade replaces the synthetic helper with a real photometric-analysis run; the lighting-layout example now consumes a production cascade source and the synthetic helper is deleted.

sections.title: Grid resolution derivation
sections.summary: Task area bounds 200..2500 × 200..1900 mm (2300 × 1700 mm) after 200 mm border substitution. The BS EN 12464-1 §4.3 default 500 mm border would leave a 1700 × 1100 mm task area with degenerate spacing — too small for the §6.2 adaptive formula to fit a meaningful grid. 200 mm border is the engineer's standard small-room convention. Adaptive formula gives p ≈ 356 mm snapped UP to 450 mm nominal; 6 cols × 5 rows = 30 grid points fitted to bounds (x-step 460, y-step 425). Per [grid-spacing-rules#adaptive-formula].

sections.title: Per-point illuminance — bathroom target
sections.summary: Lumen-method headline: (2 × 1500 × 0.60 × 0.80) / 5.67 = 254 lux maintained. Achieved_avg 254 lux matches upstream lighting-layout's calculation_summary.achieved_illuminance_lux. Achieved_min 168 lux at the SE corner (2500, 1900) where wall-shadow dominates; 28 lux margin over the 140 (0.7 × 200) threshold. Achieved_max 341 lux directly under each luminaire on the 1350 mm centreline. Distribution is dominated by the synthetic Lambertian IES; per-point grid is smooth-but-tight on the perimeter.

sections.title: UGR per CIE 117
sections.summary: 4 default observers per [ugr-rules#default-observer-positions] — bathroom is small enough that engineer overrides are typically unnecessary. Max UGR 18.2 (auto_S_wall observer at (1350, 400) facing the L01 + L02 pair from inside the splash zone). 19 limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.36 bathroom_domestic; PASS with 0.8 margin. The S-wall observer is the worst-case because both luminaires sit roughly on its sight-line.

sections.title: Reflectance basis
sections.summary: Ceiling 0.5 (gloss-painted, not the office-typical 0.7 because bathroom ceilings carry steam staining over time); walls 0.5 (light tiled, mid-gloss); floor 0.2 (mid-tone tile). CIBSE SLL Code for Lighting 2012 standard reflectance table for low-RI tiled bathroom — matches upstream lighting-layout UF = 0.60 at RI = 0.79.

sections.title: Cascade signal to lighting-layout INV-11
sections.summary: Intent payload sets task_area_compliant=true; non_compliance_flags=[]; verification_status_lowest=synthetic_reference_C3. When upstream lighting-layout INV-11 reads this: sub-check 1 source_path present PASS; sub-check 2 task_area_compliant=true PASS; sub-check 3 achieved_avg 254 ≥ target 200 PASS, UGR 18.2 ≤ 19 PASS, U₀ 0.66 ≥ 0.4 PASS; sub-check 4 no flags to cascade PASS. lighting-layout INV-11 PASS HIGH.

sections.title: Honest disclosure — synthetic IES
sections.summary: IES file is shared/photometric/ies/LED_DOWNLIGHT.ies (verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3) — NOT manufacturer-measured. The 1500 lm scaling matches the upstream LED_DOWNLIGHT_IPX4 ontology default. Engineer-of-record must substitute project-specific IPx4 manufacturer IES (Aurora, JCC, Collingwood IPx4 series, etc.) before final design freeze per [ies-provenance-rules#substitution-policy]. The 200 mm border substitution for the 500 mm default is documented here so any audit can trace the deviation from BS EN 12464-1 §4.3.

invariants.evidence: Achieved min 168 lux ≥ 0.7 × target 200 = 140 lux per BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.36 + §4.4. Cold point at SE corner (2500, 1900); 28 lux margin over threshold.

Achieved U₀ = 168/254 = 0.661 ≥ 0.4 bathroom target per [ugr-rules#per-room-type-limits] bathroom row (Table 5.36 bathroom_domestic). Spatial distribution from synthetic Lambertian IPx4 IES is smooth across the 30-point grid.

Max UGR 18.2 (auto_S_wall observer at (1350, 400)) ≤ 19 bathroom limit per BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.36 bathroom_domestic. 0.8 margin. Worst-case observer sits on the L01 + L02 sight-line from inside the splash zone.

1 distinct luminaire_type (LED_DOWNLIGHT_IPX4) in upstream lighting-layout consumed_summary; 1 ies_files[] entry matches; LM-63 parser returns total_lumens=1500 + max_intensity_cd=1295 + beam_angle_deg=60.
grid_spacing_mm=450 matches BS EN 12464-1:2021 §6.2 formula output p ≈ 356 mm snapped UP to fit the 6 × 5 = 30 point grid exactly across the 2300 × 1700 mm task area. d = 2.3 m longest side.
illuminance_grid.length (full runtime emission) = point_count=30. 10-sample slice in this IR; every (x_mm, y_mm) inside task_area_bounds [200,2500]×[200,1900]. No duplicate coordinates.

4 ugr_results entries with _source=cie_117_default (N, S, E, W wall-facing per [ugr-rules#default-observer-positions]). No engineer overrides supplied in inputs.ugr_view_positions_override[] — bathroom is too small to warrant additional observer positions.
ies_files[0]._source length=359 chars ≥ 40 minimum; verification_status=synthetic_reference_C3 in enum per [ies-provenance-rules#verification-status-enum]; engineer-substitution note explicit.
